ENSO Diagnostic Discussion
issued by
CLIMATE PREDICTION CENTER/NCEP/NWS
and the International Research Institute for Climate and Society
8 May 2026


ENSO Alert System Status: Not Active

Synopsis:  ENSO-neutral conditions are favored to persist through
the Northern Hemisphere summer 2026, with chances of La Niña
increasing later in the year.

During April 2026, equatorial sea surface temperatures (SSTs) were
near average across most of the Pacific Ocean.  The latest weekly Niño
indices were +0.1°C in Niño-3.4, +0.2°C in Niño-3, +0.2°C in Niño-4,
and -0.1°C in Niño-1+2.

A multi-model ensemble of ENSO predictions indicates that
ENSO-neutral is the most likely outcome through summer, with
La Niña becoming more likely in the second half of 2026.


CPC/IRI PROBABILISTIC ENSO OUTLOOK
This forecast updates the previous outlook issued on 10 April 2026
and is based on a consensus of CPC and IRI assessments.

Season    La Niña    Neutral    El Niño
AMJ 2026      8         78         14
MJJ 2026     15         70         15
JJA 2026     23         63         14
JAS 2026     28         57         15
ASO 2026     32         53         15
SON 2026     33         51         16
OND 2026     33         50         17
NDJ 2026     32         50         18
DJF 2026     31         51         18


The next ENSO Diagnostic Discussion is scheduled for 12 June 2026.
Climate Prediction Center
National Centers for Environmental Prediction
NOAA/National Weather Service
College Park, MD 20740
